title: Arsenal's William Saliba to miss extended period with back injury, avoids surgery
dek: Arsenal centre-back William Saliba will not require surgery but faces an extended absence following assessments of a chronic back injury sustained during France's World Cup semifinal. The 25-year-old, who played four matches for France before the injury, will begin a managed recovery programme immediately.
author: The GoCheckMySports Desk
key_fact: Saliba will miss an extended period without surgical intervention, though no specific timeline has been disclosed.
bottom_line: Saliba's return date remains unspecified, leaving uncertainty around Arsenal's defensive depth for the opening weeks of the season. Watch for updates on his recovery timeline and which defender Arsenal deploys alongside their other centre-back options.
body: Arsenal centre-back William Saliba will not require surgery but will be sidelined for an extended period following assessments of a chronic back injury, the club announced. Extensive evaluations concluded that surgery is not recommended, and Saliba will begin a managed recovery programme with continual injury management, according to Arsenal's statement.

The 25-year-old defender sustained the injury during France's World Cup semifinal against Spain, which he left early. He subsequently missed France's third-place playoff against England. Saliba had been nursing the back injury going into the World Cup but started four matches in France's route to the semifinal before his condition worsened.

Arsenal made 50 appearances in all competitions last season with Saliba as a regular starter, helping the club win the Premier League and reach the Champions League final, per ESPN's reporting. His absence creates a gap in the club's defensive lineup as it prepares to defend its league title.

The club has not disclosed a specific timeline for Saliba's return. Arsenal begin the defence of their Premier League title on August 22 when they take on Coventry City at the Emirates Stadium, less than a month away. The managed recovery programme will commence immediately, though the scope and duration of his absence remain undefined.
Arsenal has not detailed who will fill the centre-back role during Saliba's recovery.
